RequestBody.input: What are the latest trends in renewable energy?

content.text:
Here are key trends shaping the renewable energy sector in 2026:

1. **Energy Storage Innovations**: Advances in battery technologies, such as lithium-silicon and sodium-ion, are enabling longer-duration energy storage systems, which help mitigate the intermittency of solar and wind power. Grid-scale storage projects are expanding rapidly to ensure 24/7 renewable power availability [Renewable Energy Conference](https://www.renewableenergyconference.org/blog/trends-in-renewable-energy-2026.html).

2. **Solar Power Advancements**: Technologies like perovskite solar cells are becoming commercially viable, offering higher efficiency at lower costs. Floating solar farms and solar-integrated building materials (BIPVs) are gaining traction globally, with significant growth expected across Asia and Europe [pv Magazine USA](https://pv-magazine-usa.com/2025/10/30/five-trends-that-will-shape-renewable-energy-in-2026/).

3. **Green Hydrogen Expansion**: Electrolyzer costs are reducing, making green hydrogen a feasible option for industries like steel and aviation. More countries are establishing national hydrogen strategies, with hydrogen hubs emerging globally [RatedPower](https://www.renewableenergymagazine.com).

4. **Grid Modernization**: Aging infrastructure remains a bottleneck for scaling renewables. Investment in smarter and resilient grid technologies, such as virtual power plants and AI-enhanced systems, is being prioritized to optimize energy distribution and management [S&P Global Energy](https://press.spglobal.com/2025-12-09-S-P-Global-Energy-Releases-Key-Clean-Energy-Trends-for-2026-as-AI-Growth-and-Geopolitical-Shifts-Reshape-Global-Energy-Markets).

5. **Offshore Wind Expansion**: Floating wind farm technologies are allowing access to deep-water sites with stronger winds. This trend is expected to boost wind energy capacity significantly, particularly in the North Sea and Asia-Pacific regions [Renewable Energy Conference](https://www.renewableenergyconference.org/blog/trends-in-renewable-energy-2026.html).

6. **AI and Digitalization**: Artificial intelligence and IoT are improving predictive analytics and energy optimization, enhancing smart grid functionality. Technologies like blockchain are helping decentralize energy markets for improved transparency [StartUs Insights](https://www.startus-insights.com/innovators-guide/renewable-energy-trends/).

7. **Geopolitical Shifts**: Policy changes, like competitive bidding replacing fixed pricing for renewables in countries such as China, are influencing global capacities. China is projected to slow down solar installations but maintain leadership in cumulative capacity growth [S&P Global Energy](https://press.spglobal.com/2025-12-09-S-P-Global-Energy-Releases-Key-Clean-Energy-Trends-for-2026-as-AI-Growth-and-Geopolitical-Shifts-Reshape-Global-Energy-Markets).

8. **Decentralized Energy Systems**: A shift towards localized energy solutions, such as microgrids and community renewable energy projects, is promoting energy independence while reducing grid congestion [StartUs Insights](https://www.startus-insights.com/innovators-guide/renewable-energy-trends/).

These trends are driving the transition to cleaner, more sustainable global energy systems while tackling challenges like infrastructure and policy limitations.
